header.label: Stage 6 Phase 6 — Ticket WW (complete_task data-loss fix + regression tests)

scope.summary:
Phase 6 closes Ticket WW (id 318, priority=2/MEDIUM): Fix the Vikunja MCP server's complete_task data-loss bug (anomaly A33) and add regression tests.
Two sub-items:
(i) CODE FIX — Replace complete_task in devplatform/tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py (line 447-454) with a read-then-write implementation that preserves title, description, and priority before marking done=True. The current implementation sends only {"done": True} to POST /tasks/{id}, causing Vikunja to zero all absent fields (description, priority) via Go zero-value defaults.
(ii) REGRESSION TESTS — Add a new test file devplatform/tools/vikunja_mcp/tests/test_server_ww.py following the existing mock pattern in test_server_f5.py. Tests must cover: (a) complete_task preserves description + priority across the call, (b) complete_task issues GET before POST (ordering assertion), (c) update_task sends only non-None fields (parity/regression guard), (d) complete_task 401-retry behaviour (matches existing contract).
SECONDARY DEFECT CHECK (sub-item i prerequisite): The Ticket WW description also notes a secondary path-string defect: hardcoded "\tasks\" (Python tab-escape) somewhere in server.py. Guide-#10 inspection of current HEAD found NO such backslash path — all paths use forward slashes (/tasks/). EA must verify this independently (see Check 0e). If the defect is absent, document "already correct" and skip the fix. Do NOT invent a fix for a defect that is not present.

ticket_ww.title: Stage 6.7.5 Ticket WW: Fix Vikunja MCP server complete_task data-loss bug + path defect
ticket_ww.acceptance: 1. complete_task invocation on a task with non-empty description + non-zero priority does NOT zero those fields; readback via get_task confirms preservation. 2. Path string confirmed: no "\tasks\" (Python tab-escape) present in server.py, or fix applied if found. 3. Regression test (a): description + priority preservation across complete_task. 4. Regression test (b): GET issued before POST in complete_task. 5. Regression test (c): update_task sends only non-None fields. 6. Regression test (d): complete_task 401-retry behaviour. 7. All existing tests in devplatform/tools/vikunja_mcp/tests/ still pass. 8. STATUS.md E14 entry records Phase 6 complete.

scope.operational_impact:
After Phase 6 merges and the MCP server restarts (at next Claude Code session boundary), mcp__vikunja__complete_task is restored to safe operation. The A33 Option B workaround (get_task → update_task(done=True, all fields) → add_task_comment) becomes optional scaffolding rather than a mandatory safety gate. Phases 7+ may use complete_task directly.
IMPORTANT — CHICKEN-AND-EGG CONSTRAINT: EA-15 CANNOT use the fixed complete_task to close Ticket WW (318) or the Phase 6 tracking task during this phase. The fix exists on a feature branch; the running MCP server still uses the old server.py. Use Option B (get_task → update_task(done=True, all fields) → add_task_comment) for ALL Vikunja closes in Phase 6, exactly as in prior phases.
MCP-403-P5 NOTE: If the MCP server in EA-15's session returns 403 (stale VIKUNJA_PASS, same issue as EA-14), fall back to REST API for all Vikunja writes per the LA-authorized REST fallback precedent (MCP-403-P5). Document in close report.

background.defect_analysis:
File: devplatform/tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py lines 446-454 (current):
@mcp.tool() def complete_task(task_id: int) -> str: """Mark a task as done.
Args: task_id: The task ID to complete. """ task = _api_post(f"/tasks/{task_id}", {"done": True}) return json.dumps({"id": task["id"], "title": task["title"], "done": True}, indent=2)
Root cause: _api_post calls _request_with_retry("POST", path, json={"done": True}). The Vikunja v2.3.0 backend interprets POST /tasks/{id} as a full-object update. Any field absent from the request body is overwritten with its Go zero-value: - description: "" (empty string) - priority: 0 (unset) Labels and comments survive (stored in separate join tables). Title is protected by server-side required-field validation.
Impact: any task with a non-empty description or non-zero priority that is closed via mcp__vikunja__complete_task silently loses both fields. This is the data-loss pattern observed in anomaly A33 (EA-10 Phase 1, 2026-05-07).
Workaround in use since A33 (Phases 1-5): Option B — callers call get_task to read current title/description/priority, then update_task(task_id, title=..., description=..., priority=..., done=True), then add_task_comment.

The Ticket WW description notes a secondary defect: hardcoded "\tasks\" (Python tab-escape) in path construction, where \t is a tab character.
Guide-#10 inspection of current HEAD (2026-05-08): grep for backslash-tasks across server.py: NO MATCHES FOUND. All path strings in current HEAD use forward slashes (/tasks/...).
Conclusion: secondary defect CANNOT BE CONFIRMED in current HEAD. Either it was pre-corrected in a prior commit, or it was a misidentification at ticket creation. EA must independently verify (see Check 0e) before treating as in-scope. If not found: document "secondary defect not present in current HEAD" in close report and skip. If found: fix in the same C1 commit as the primary fix.
background.fix_rationale:
Fix for complete_task: change to read-then-write pattern, matching the Option B workaround that has been proven safe across Phases 1-5.
NEW implementation: current = _api_get(f"/tasks/{task_id}") body = { "title": current["title"], "description": current.get("description") or "", "priority": current.get("priority", 0), "done": True, } task = _api_post(f"/tasks/{task_id}", body) return json.dumps({"id": task["id"], "title": task["title"], "done": True}, indent=2)
Why "or """" for description: Vikunja may return null or absent description; the or "" guard ensures the POST body always sends a string (not null/None), matching Vikunja's expected type.
Why keep priority as int (not conditional): priority=0 is the valid "unset" value per CLAUDE.md (priority scale 0=unset); sending priority=0 when the task has priority=0 is correct behaviour.
update_task is NOT affected: it already sends only non-None kwargs (lines 420-432), so update_task(task_id, done=True) would also only send {"done": True}. However, the Option B caller pattern always passes title/description/priority explicitly, which is why Option B is safe. update_task itself is not being changed.
background.test_infrastructure:
Existing test file: devplatform/tools/vikunja_mcp/tests/test_server_f5.py Pattern: 1. @pytest.fixture(autouse=True) seeds server._token = "test-jwt-token" so _get_token() returns the cached value without hitting /login. 2. patch.object(server.httpx, "request", return_value=mock_response) intercepts all HTTP calls routed through _request_with_retry. 3. For multi-call sequences (e.g., 401-retry), side_effect=[resp1, resp2] is used. 4. Assert on mock_req.call_args (for single call) or call_args_list (for sequence).
New file: devplatform/tools/vikunja_mcp/tests/test_server_ww.py Must import: json, from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call import pytest, from tools.vikunja_mcp import server
For complete_task tests, httpx.request is called TWICE (GET then POST) in the fixed implementation. Use side_effect=[get_response, post_response].

comprehension_gate.instruction: Respond with a comprehension gate covering CG1-CG7 exactly. Stop after the gate. Wait for Guide APPROVED before any pre-flight or edit.

items.item: State Ticket WW's id, priority, and its two sub-items. For each sub-item, state what type of work it is (code fix / test addition) and which file(s) are affected. State the operational impact after Phase 6 merges and the MCP server restarts.

Explain the primary defect in concrete terms: - What does the current complete_task (line 447-454) send to the API? - What does the Vikunja backend do with absent fields? - Which fields survive and which are zeroed? - Why does the Option B workaround (used in Phases 1-5) avoid this problem?

Describe the fix precisely: - What is the exact OLD complete_task body (lines 447-454)? - What is the exact NEW complete_task body? - Why is "or """ used for the description field? - Is update_task being changed? Why or why not? - State the chicken-and-egg constraint: which Option B or REST fallback must EA-15 use for closing Ticket WW (318) and the Phase 6 tracking task?

Describe the secondary defect check (sub-item i prerequisite): - What does the ticket say the secondary defect is? - What did Guide-#10's inspection find in current HEAD? - What exact command does EA-15 run to independently verify presence? - What are the two possible dispositions (found vs. not found)?

Describe the four regression tests in test_server_ww.py: For each test: name, what it mocks, what it asserts. Explain how httpx.request is mocked for a two-call sequence (GET then POST) in the complete_task tests.

State the commit plan: branch name, how many commits, which repo each lands in. State the A33 workaround status for Phase 6 Vikunja closes (WW 318 and tracking task). State the expected backlog after Phase 6 closes.

Confirm all 8 binding acks (g10-ea15_n1 through n8) are understood. For n3 (no-amend doctrine) confirm which 4 frozen verdict files must not be touched. For n4 (chicken-and-egg) confirm what EA-15 uses for WW (318) and tracking task closes. For n7 (post-merge status) confirm what changes for Phase 7+ and what does not change during Phase 6 itself.

preflight.check: Read C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\autonomy_budget\state.json. Confirm fleet_paused=false. If fleet_paused=true: HARD STOP.

BlarAI: git -C "C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI" tag --list EXPECT: pre-platform-extract AND post-platform-extract present. platform-extracted is NOT a BlarAI tag — its absence is not a defect. devplatform: git -C "C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform" tag --list EXPECT: platform-extracted present (at ae75507). If any required tag absent: HARD STOP.

check.label: Confirm server.py line 453 defect present (pre-fix baseline)

preflight.check: Run from devplatform: python -c " import inspect from tools.vikunja_mcp import server src = inspect.getsource(server.complete_task) print(src) print() print('Defect present?' , '{\"done\": True}' in src.replace('\"done\"', '\"done\"')) " EXPECT: source shows _api_post(f"/tasks/{task_id}", {"done": True}). This confirms the primary defect is present and the fix is needed. If the body already includes description/priority reads: HARD STOP — investigate before proceeding (fix may have been partially applied).
Run from devplatform: python -m pytest tools/vikunja_mcp/tests/ -v --tb=short -q EXPECT: all existing tests pass. If any fail pre-fix: HARD STOP. Record baseline pass count in close report.

check.label: Secondary defect check — backslash path verification

preflight.check: Run from devplatform: python -c " from pathlib import Path src = Path('tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py').read_text(encoding='utf-8') hits = [i for i, line in enumerate(src.splitlines(), 1) if chr(9) in line and 'tasks' in line.lower()] print(f'Tab+tasks lines: {hits}') print('Secondary defect present?' , bool(hits)) " ALSO run (grep alternative): python -c " import re from pathlib import Path src = Path('tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py').read_text(encoding='utf-8') # search for literal backslash-t in string literals (not just the tab character) hits = [(i+1, l) for i, l in enumerate(src.splitlines()) if r'\tasks' in l or r'\t' in l] for ln, l in hits: print(f' line {ln}: {l!r}') print('Raw backslash-t found:', bool(hits)) " If either check finds a tab-character-tasks or literal-backslash-t in a path string: - Fix in C1 alongside the primary fix. - Document line number and fix in close report. If no hits: document "secondary defect not present in current HEAD" and move on.
preflight.fleet_pause:
Pause the fleet using the Python helper: python -c "from tools.autonomy_budget import state; state.pause_fleet('Stage 6 Phase 6 Ticket WW complete_task fix', updated_by='ea_code', path='C:/Users/mrbla/devplatform/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json')"
Commit immediately in devplatform (on main, before branching): git -C C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform add tools/autonomy_budget/state.json git -C C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform commit -m "chore(ops): pause fleet -- Stage 6 Phase 6 Ticket WW"
preflight.tracking_task:
Create Phase 6 tracking task in Vikunja Project 10 (DevPlatform-Meta): Title: "Stage 6.7.5 Phase 6 tracking: Ticket WW (complete_task data-loss fix + regression tests)" Priority: 2 Labels: Active (id 1), Architecture (id 4), Testing (id 6) Add starting comment: "[agent:ea_code][phase:start] Phase 6 execution started. Scope: Ticket WW (318). Pre-flight checks passed. Chicken-and-egg constraint acknowledged: Option B / REST fallback for all Phase 6 Vikunja closes." Record tracking task ID for PF1 bifurcation at close.
VIKUNJA MCP NOTE: If MCP returns 403 (stale VIKUNJA_PASS), use REST fallback per MCP-403-P5 precedent (LA-authorized). Document in close report.

execution.branch_setup: Create and checkout a feature branch in devplatform: git -C C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform checkout -b feat/stage6-phase6-ww-complete-task-fix

edit.instruction:
Replace the current complete_task function body (lines 446-454):
CURRENT (DO NOT KEEP): @mcp.tool() def complete_task(task_id: int) -> str: """Mark a task as done.
Args: task_id: The task ID to complete. """ task = _api_post(f"/tasks/{task_id}", {"done": True}) return json.dumps({"id": task["id"], "title": task["title"], "done": True}, indent=2)
NEW (REPLACE WITH): @mcp.tool() def complete_task(task_id: int) -> str: """Mark a task as done.
Reads current title, description, and priority before posting, so that Vikunja's full-object POST semantics do not zero absent fields (A33 fix).
Args: task_id: The task ID to complete. """ current = _api_get(f"/tasks/{task_id}") body = { "title": current["title"], "description": current.get("description") or "", "priority": current.get("priority", 0), "done": True, } task = _api_post(f"/tasks/{task_id}", body) return json.dumps({"id": task["id"], "title": task["title"], "done": True}, indent=2)
Also apply secondary defect fix if and only if Check 0e found a backslash-path or tab-character-tasks hit. If no hit: do not touch any other lines.
VERIFY after edit: python -c " import inspect from tools.vikunja_mcp import server src = inspect.getsource(server.complete_task) assert '_api_get' in src, 'GET call missing from fixed complete_task' assert 'description' in src, 'description preservation missing' assert 'priority' in src, 'priority preservation missing' print('VERIFY PASS: complete_task contains GET + description + priority') print(src) " EXPECT: VERIFY PASS printed, source shows the read-then-write pattern.

new_file.instruction:
Create a new test file following the mock pattern from test_server_f5.py. The file must contain exactly these 4 tests (plus the autouse fixture and imports):
FILE CONTENT (verbatim structure — adapt docstrings freely):
""" Regression tests for Ticket WW fixes in tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py.
WW primary fix: complete_task read-then-write (GET before POST) to prevent Vikunja's full-object POST semantics from zeroing description + priority.
All HTTP calls are mocked — no real Vikunja dependency. Pattern mirrors tools/vikunja_mcp/tests/test_server_f5.py. """ from __future__ import annotations
import json from unittest.mock import MagicMock, call, patch
import pytest
from tools.vikunja_mcp import server
@pytest.fixture(autouse=True) def _seed_token(): server._token = "test-jwt-token" yield server._token = None
def test_complete_task_preserves_description_and_priority(): """complete_task must not zero description or priority (A33 regression guard).""" get_resp = MagicMock() get_resp.status_code = 200 get_resp.json.return_value = { "id": 42, "title": "My task", "description": "Important context", "priority": 3, "done": False, } post_resp = MagicMock() post_resp.status_code = 200 post_resp.json.return_value = { "id": 42, "title": "My task", "description": "Important context", "priority": 3, "done": True, }
with patch.object(server.httpx, "request", side_effect=[get_resp, post_resp]) as mock_req: result_str = server.complete_task(task_id=42)
assert mock_req.call_count == 2 post_call = mock_req.call_args_list[1] posted_body = post_call.kwargs.get("json") or post_call[1].get("json", {}) assert posted_body.get("description") == "Important context", ( "description must be preserved in POST body" ) assert posted_body.get("priority") == 3, ( "priority must be preserved in POST body" ) assert posted_body.get("done") is True parsed = json.loads(result_str) assert parsed["id"] == 42 assert parsed["done"] is True
def test_complete_task_issues_get_before_post(): """complete_task must GET the task first, then POST with done=True.""" get_resp = MagicMock() get_resp.status_code = 200 get_resp.json.return_value = { "id": 7, "title": "Task seven", "description": "", "priority": 0, "done": False, } post_resp = MagicMock() post_resp.status_code = 200 post_resp.json.return_value = { "id": 7, "title": "Task seven", "description": "", "priority": 0, "done": True, }
with patch.object(server.httpx, "request", side_effect=[get_resp, post_resp]) as mock_req: server.complete_task(task_id=7)
calls = mock_req.call_args_list assert len(calls) == 2 get_method, get_url = calls[0][0][0], calls[0][0][1] post_method, post_url = calls[1][0][0], calls[1][0][1] assert get_method == "GET", f"First call must be GET, got {get_method}" assert get_url.endswith("/tasks/7"), f"GET URL must end with /tasks/7, got {get_url!r}" assert post_method == "POST", f"Second call must be POST, got {post_method}" assert post_url.endswith("/tasks/7"), f"POST URL must end with /tasks/7, got {post_url!r}"
def test_update_task_sends_only_non_none_fields(): """update_task must not include None-valued fields in the POST body (parity guard).""" mock_resp = MagicMock() mock_resp.status_code = 200 mock_resp.json.return_value = { "id": 10, "title": "Updated title", "done": False, "priority": 0, "percent_done": 0.0, }
with patch.object(server.httpx, "request", return_value=mock_resp) as mock_req: server.update_task(task_id=10, title="Updated title")
posted_body = mock_req.call_args.kwargs.get("json") or mock_req.call_args[1].get("json", {}) assert "title" in posted_body, "title must be in POST body when supplied" assert "description" not in posted_body, ( "description must NOT be in POST body when not supplied (None)" ) assert "priority" not in posted_body, ( "priority must NOT be in POST body when not supplied (None)" )
def test_complete_task_retries_on_401(): """On 401 from the GET call, complete_task re-authenticates and retries.""" unauthorized = MagicMock() unauthorized.status_code = 401
get_ok = MagicMock() get_ok.status_code = 200 get_ok.json.return_value = { "id": 5, "title": "Retry task", "description": "desc", "priority": 1, "done": False, } post_ok = MagicMock() post_ok.status_code = 200 post_ok.json.return_value = { "id": 5, "title": "Retry task", "description": "desc", "priority": 1, "done": True, } login_resp = MagicMock() login_resp.status_code = 200 login_resp.json.return_value = {"token": "refreshed-jwt"} login_resp.raise_for_status = lambda: None
call_seq = [unauthorized, get_ok, post_ok]
def _side_effect(*args, **kwargs): return call_seq.pop(0)
with patch.object(server.httpx, "request", side_effect=_side_effect) as mock_req: with patch.object(server.httpx, "post", return_value=login_resp) as mock_post: result_str = server.complete_task(task_id=5)
assert mock_req.call_count == 3, ( "Expected 3 httpx.request calls: 401 GET, retry GET, POST" ) mock_post.assert_called_once() parsed = json.loads(result_str) assert parsed["id"] == 5 assert parsed["done"] is True
VERIFY after creating the file by running tests: python -m pytest tools/vikunja_mcp/tests/test_server_ww.py -v --tb=short EXPECT: 4 tests collected, 4 passed. If any test fails: HARD STOP. Fix the test logic before committing.
Also run the full suite to confirm no regressions: python -m pytest tools/vikunja_mcp/tests/ -v --tb=short -q EXPECT: all tests pass (pre-fix baseline count + 4 new).

execution.commit:
Stage exactly the 2 changed files (explicit-add): git -C C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform add tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py git -C C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform add tools/vikunja_mcp/tests/test_server_ww.py
If secondary defect was found and fixed, server.py is already staged above. No additional files.
Commit message: "fix(vikunja_mcp): Ticket WW -- complete_task read-then-write; preserve description + priority (A33)"
Do NOT use git add . or git add -A.

vikunja_close.label: Close Ticket WW (id 318) — Option B or REST

execution.vikunja_close: CONSTRAINT: complete_task MCP tool is still suspended during Phase 6 (MCP server uses old server.py until restart). Use Option B: 1. get_task(task_id=318) — capture title, description, priority 2. update_task(task_id=318, title=captured, description=captured, priority=captured, done=True) OR REST equivalent if MCP returns 403. 3. add_task_comment(task_id=318, comment="[agent:ea_code][phase:completion] Ticket WW complete. Sub-item (i): complete_task fixed — read-then-write pattern (GET then POST with title/description/priority preserved). A33 primary defect resolved. Secondary defect: <found/not found — state result>. Sub-item (ii): 4 regression tests in test_server_ww.py — all PASS. devplatform commit: <SHA of C1>. After MCP server restart, complete_task is safe to use directly.")

edit.instruction:
1. Update Stage 6 table row to add Phase 6 SHAs.
2. Append evolution entry E14 to the Stage 6 evolution log section: "Stage 6 Phase 6 (Ticket WW) complete. Sub-item (i): complete_task data-loss bug (A33) fixed in devplatform/tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py -- changed from POST-only-done=True to read-then-write (GET title/description/ priority → POST with all fields + done=True). Secondary defect (backslash path): <not found in current HEAD / found at line N and fixed>. Sub-item (ii): 4 regression tests added in test_server_ww.py -- all PASS. After MCP server restart, mcp__vikunja__complete_task restored to safe operation; A33 Option B workaround deprecated. Backlog: 43 total / 24 done / 19 open."
Commit in BlarAI on main: git -C C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI add docs/platform_separation/STATUS.md git -C C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI commit -m "docs(stage6_phase6): record Phase 6 complete in STATUS.md (E14)"

execution.merge:
In devplatform, merge to main (no-ff): git -C C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform checkout main git -C C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform merge --no-ff feat/stage6-phase6-ww-complete-task-fix -m "fix(vikunja_mcp): Stage 6 Phase 6 -- complete_task A33 fix + regression tests (Ticket WW)"
Record merge commit SHA for close report.
execution.fleet_unpause:
Unpause using resume_fleet (NOT unpause_fleet — AttributeError): python -c "from tools.autonomy_budget import state; state.resume_fleet(updated_by='ea_code', path='C:/Users/mrbla/devplatform/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json')"
Commit in devplatform on main: git -C C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform add tools/autonomy_budget/state.json git -C C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform commit -m "chore(ops): unpause fleet -- Stage 6 Phase 6 complete"
execution.pf1_bifurcation:
PASS path (all 4 tests pass, WW closed, STATUS.md committed, merge done, fleet unpaused): Close Phase 6 tracking task via Option B / REST (get_task → update_task(done=True, all fields preserved) → add_task_comment).
HARD STOP path (any test failed, or VERIFY check failed): Add comment to tracking task with failure summary. Leave done=false. Per AOAM §4.

ea_dispatch.close_report_format:
Write close report to: C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\platform_separation\temp_for_responses\6.7.5-EA_INSTANCE_15_STAGE6_PHASE_6_CLOSE_REPORT.md
Report must cover: 1. Phase 6 PASS / HARD STOP verdict 2. Pre-flight results (Check 0a/0b/0c/0d/0e) 3. Sub-item (i): exact old/new complete_task body; secondary defect disposition 4. Sub-item (ii): test output (all 4 tests), full-suite pass count 5. Commit SHAs: pause, C1, STATUS.md, merge, unpause (both repos) 6. Vikunja: Ticket 318 done=?, tracking task done=? 7. Post-merge operational status: complete_task restored (pending MCP restart) 8. Backlog: total / done / open
Post short summary (5-10 lines) to chat.

constraints.constraint: complete_task MCP tool remains SUSPENDED for Phase 6 Vikunja closes. The fix lands on a feature branch; the running MCP server still uses old code. Use Option B (get_task → update_task(done=True, fields preserved) → add_task_comment) for Ticket WW (318) and the Phase 6 tracking task. After Phase 6 merge and MCP server restart (next Claude Code session), complete_task is safe. Document the restored status in the close report.

Four frozen verdict files must NOT be edited (no-amend doctrine): docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/3.0.v2-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_7_STAGE3_COMPREHENSIVE_AUDIT_CHECKPOINT.xml docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/3.1.v2-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_7_ITEM_3_1_V2_VERIFIED_SPEC_CORRECTION_AUTHORIZED.xml docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/3.2.v2-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_7_ITEM_3_2_V2_RECLASSIFICATION_REQUIRED_SELECTIVE_COPY_AUTHORIZED.xml docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/3.10-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_7_STAGE3_CLOSE_VERIFIED.xml
All git add commands name files explicitly. Never git add . or git add -A.

Only 2 files change in C1: server.py and test_server_ww.py (plus server.py secondary defect fix if Check 0e finds a hit). Do not modify test_server_f5.py or any other existing test. Do not touch update_task — it is not broken. If additional defects are found during review, document in close report and defer to Stage 6.7.5 backlog — do not expand scope.
The 4 regression tests in test_server_ww.py must all pass before C1. If a test cannot be made to pass, HARD STOP and document the failure. Do not commit a test that is known to fail.

exclusions.item: I (283), FF (296), GG (297), HH (298) and all other open tickets — future phases.
update_task logic change — not broken; not in scope.
A33 retrospective fixups — prior Vikunja closes using Option B are correct and complete; no retroactive re-close needed.

MCP server restart — EA-15 cannot restart the Claude Code MCP server. The restart happens at the next session boundary (user action). Document "pending restart" in close report; do not attempt to restart.

acknowledgements.ack: Fleet-pause SOP: state.pause_fleet(...) + commit BEFORE branch checkout; state.resume_fleet(...) (NOT unpause_fleet) + commit as LAST action.
Explicit-add discipline: all git add commands name files explicitly. Never git add . or git add -A.
No-amend doctrine: 4 frozen verdict files (3.0.v2, 3.1.v2, 3.2.v2, 3.10) must not be edited. These are in docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/.

Chicken-and-egg: complete_task MCP tool remains SUSPENDED for Phase 6 closes. Ticket WW (318) and Phase 6 tracking task must be closed via Option B (get_task → update_task(done=True, all fields) → add_task_comment) or REST fallback (if MCP-403). After merge + MCP restart, complete_task is restored.

Check 0b is two-repo: BlarAI must have pre-platform-extract + post-platform-extract. devplatform must have platform-extracted at ae75507. platform-extracted is NOT a BlarAI tag — its absence from BlarAI is not a defect.

Secondary defect (backslash path): verify presence via Check 0e before any fix. Guide-#10 inspection found no hit in current HEAD. If Check 0e also finds no hit: document "not present" and skip. Never invent a fix for an absent defect.

Post-merge status: after Phase 6 merge + MCP server restart, complete_task is safe. During Phase 6 itself, Option B remains mandatory. Close report must explicitly state "complete_task restored — pending MCP server restart."
PF1 bifurcation per AOAM §4: PASS path closes tracking task (done=True, Option B); HARD STOP path adds comment + leaves done=false.

guide_verification.check: devplatform: git log --oneline HEAD shows unpause → merge → C1 → pause (reverse chrono)

devplatform: git show C1_SHA -- tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py confirms: - complete_task now calls _api_get before _api_post - body includes title, description, priority, done=True - no other functions changed
devplatform: git show C1_SHA -- tools/vikunja_mcp/tests/test_server_ww.py confirms 4 test functions present
devplatform: pytest tools/vikunja_mcp/tests/ passes (all pre-existing + 4 new)
BlarAI: git log --oneline HEAD shows STATUS.md E14 commit
Vikunja: backlog shows 43 total / 24 done / 19 open
guide_verification.backlog_expectation: After Phase 5 + Ticket XX creation: 42 total / 22 done / 20 open Phase 6: +1 total (tracking task) / +2 done (WW 318 + tracking task) / -1 open (WW 318) After Phase 6: 43 total / 24 done / 19 open
